LAW AND MOTION, HEARING ON JUDGMENT DEBTOR CHRISTINA J JOHNSON'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION THE CLAIM OF EXEMPTION IS GRANTED IN PART. THE COURT CONCLUDES THAT THE ACCOUNT CONTAINED "PAID EARNINGS" THAT ARE EXEMPT PER CCP 704.070(B)(2). THE COURT FINDS THAT $1,482.07 OF THE MONEY LEVIED UPON IS EXEMPT. THE CREDITOR SHALL RETURN THAT SUM TO DEFENDANT AS THE PAPERS INDICATE THAT THE SHERIFF IS NOT HOLDING THE FUNDS. THE COURT WILL SIGN AND MAIL OUT A FORMAL ORDER TO THE PARTIES AND TO THE SHERIFF'S OFFICE. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: SEAN KANE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED (302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Aug-31-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Steve Levy, Esq. 310-289-5177 of the Law Offices of Kenosian & Miele for Plaintiff PMGI by Zoom.
Christina Johnson, 760-521-4050 Defendant in Pro Per. (appears in Person in the Courtroom)

RULING - A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF PMGI FINANCIAL, LLC. HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Christine Johnson's claim of exemption is granted. (See CCP 704.070(b)(2).) The levying officer is directed to release the funds held to the debtor.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Nov-09-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. ALISA KIM. ESQ. specially appearing for atty. John Kenosian of Law Offices of Kenosian & Miele, LLP (Tel: 323-297-2100) for judgment creditor PMGI Financial, LLC;
No other appearances are made in this matter.

HEARING ON JUDGMENT DEBTOR CHRISTINA J JOHNSON'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION

The matter is uncontested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. The claim of exemption is GRANTED IN PART. The court concludes that the account contained "paid earnings" that are exempt per CCP 704.070(b)(2). The court finds that $1,482.07 of the money levied upon is exempt. The creditor shall return that sum to defendant as the papers indicate that the Sheriff is not holding the funds. The Court will sign and mail out a formal order to the parties and to the Sheriff's Office. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: Sean Kane, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported (302/RBU)
